title: Delicate Dense Slovakian Pork Wrap
description: A pungent and cozy Slovakian dish featuring shoyu-koji and ssam-wrap, made in a fondue pot for a unique experience.
instructions: Preheat the fondue pot to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a small bowl, mix together shoyu-koji, caraway seeds, paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Rub the mixture all over the pork shoulder, making sure to coat it evenly.
Heat the coconut oil in the fondue pot and sear the pork shoulder until browned on all sides.
Add the sliced onion to the fondue pot and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic to the fondue pot and cook for an additional minute.
Wrap the cooked pork shoulder in the ssam wrap and serve with the onion and garlic mixture.
Cook the pork shoulder in the fondue pot for 10 minutes, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit.
Let the pork shoulder rest for 5 minutes before slicing and serving.
Serve the pork wrap hot, garnished with additional paprika and caraway seeds if desired.
